prose:
Computed condensed Fukui functions for benzaldehyde using the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.

Method provenance
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor; most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting)
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz coordinates (no re-optimization)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent specified)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software backend: PySCF via chemkit (fukui subcommand)
SCF convergence: achieved for the N, N+1 and N-1 states; integrity status ok (trustworthy=true)
Warnings (verbatim from engine):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live log: the calculation was executed by chemkit, which streamed its engine output to a `.out` log in the working directory while running (path available in the session’s filesystem).

Key numerical results (Mulliken population analysis):
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2) with f⁺ = 0.39938969
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.23839906

Headline value (reported under “value”): 0.39938969 — the largest f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui value) on carbon atom 3.

All data, including full per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ tables and the plot image, are stored in `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benzaldehyde_fukui.json` and the PNG path reported above. The result is fully reproducible with the `cli_invocation` recorded in that JSON.
